body.title: [pending] Consequence check: unified daemon supervisor resilience
body.intention: I intend that the Ground Loop supervisor improves daemon resilience without introducing new failure modes.
body.description:
**What changed:** All daemons consolidated under Ground Loop supervisor; file-pattern discovery, hot-reload, .borkedpy error handling.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- If Ground Loop itself crashes, all daemons stop (single point of failure; mitigated by system cron respawn)
- Runme.py imports with side effects (global state, module-level print) could interfere with hot-reload
- Config.yaml parsing error could silently skip a daemon (if error handling is too lenient)
- .Borkedpy files accumulate without cleanup if daemon never fixed
- Hot-reload timing: if daemon is logging during reload, log file could be corrupted

**Signals to watch:**
- Ground Loop process running continuously; cron/startup task restarts on reboot
- All expected runme.py files discovered on startup (logged to datacenter_logs/ground_loop/supervisor.log)
- No .borkedpy files in devices/*/groundloop/ unless daemon crashed
- Daemon logs appear at expected frequency (heartbeat for demo, requests for acurite, etc.)
- Manual rename from .borkedpy back to .py successfully reloads daemon
- No log corruption in daemon output files

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-29 (14 days) or when system has been running for 48h continuous with ≥3 daemons active.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
